era: 2007-present. Punk Pop. E=7 -- hook-driven, warm, feminist humor. Capitol Hill. Hardly Art (Sub Pop subsidiary).
labels: Sub Pop (This Mess Is a Place, 2019)
associated_musicians: Chastity Belt (Julia Shapiro, Hardly Art peer)

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST HARDLY ART entry. Sub Pop subsidiary founded 2007. Roster: Tacocat, Childbirth, Chastity Belt, La Luz, King Tuff. Sub Pop institutional range: grunge era (Mudhoney, Nirvana) to Capitol Hill punk pop (Tacocat) via Hardly Art.
theory_nodes.description: Hardly Art: Sub Pop's subsidiary, first engine entry at 282.

theory_nodes.concept: Palindrome band name (TACOCAT). Aesthetic blueprint: structural symmetry, playful wit, mundane made clever. The name signals the band's register before a note is played.
theory_nodes.description: Tacocat is a palindrome: the name as aesthetic statement.

theory_nodes.concept: Feminist humor as political strategy. 'Crimson Wave' (menstruation), 'Men Explain Things to Me' (mansplaining), 'Dana Katherine Scully' (X-Files tribute). Politics embedded in subject matter; tone is warm and humorous. Normalization-mode feminism.
theory_nodes.description: Tacocat's feminist content: politics via subject matter and hook, not manifesto.

theory_nodes.concept: Bree McKenna dual membership: Tacocat (282) + Childbirth (283). Two consecutive engine degrees share a musician. Capitol Hill micro-network documented.
theory_nodes.description: McKenna links degrees 282 and 283 through shared personnel.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 predecessor. Kill Rock Stars. Deadpan sardonic. Tacocat's warmth is the contrast: same Capitol Hill feminist punk tradition, different temperature. E rises from 6 to 7.
E=7. Hardly Art. Punk pop supergroup. Bree McKenna shared. Julia Shapiro (Chastity Belt) added. Same label, same neighborhood, consecutive degrees, shared personnel.
Riot grrrl antecedent. E=8. Confrontation-mode feminism. Tacocat's normalization-mode feminism is the contemporary inheritor of the tradition Bikini Kill established.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Pacific White-sided Dolphin: acrobatic, social, pod-based, aerial behaviors. Tacocat: hook-driven, social, warm group energy. Both bring collective exuberance at E=7. Both are exactly the right temperature for their context.
